D4 Colorimetry L4 Analyze Reference

Projecting XYZ to xy

slide the projection and watch the 3-D spectral curve flatten onto the familiar horseshoe

The chromaticity diagram is a central projection: divide each tristimulus by X+Y+Z and you collapse a 3-D cone of colours onto a 2-D plane, discarding luminance.

At the left, the spectral locus sweeps through 3-D XYZ space. Slide the projection toward 1 and watch every point fall onto the unit plane, tracing the horseshoe-shaped chromaticity boundary.
